PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Sep-07-2006 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Verna Shaheen (Conservator); Lawrence Siracusa (Counsel for Ms. Shaheen); Victor Arico (Conservatee); Kelly Dixon (Court-appointed Counsel for Mr. Arico); Carmen Cimicato (Petitioner/Nephew); Victor Cimicato (Petitioner/Nephew); Norman Macleod (Counsel for the Petitioners).

At the outset, Ms. Dixon informed the Court that Mr. Arico opposes the Petition for Appointment of Successor Conservator and the Petition for Change of Trustee and Executor because he does not remember having a past relationship with his nephews (Carmen and Victor Cimicato). Ms. Dixon stated that Mr. Arico may reconsider his nephews as successors if they are able to build a more active relationship. Mr. Macleod withdrew both Petitions without prejudice to filing in the future. Mr. Macleod noted that the nephews feel excluded by the Conservator with regards to sharing information on medical evaluations and reports and that having such information may be beneficial to their relationship with Mr. Arico. Mr. Siracusa noted that Ms. Shaheen decided that it is in Mr. Arico's best interest to stay in his home rather than placing him in an assisted living facility.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Nici Rodich #11604; Court Investigator: John Cushner; Bailiff: Mizota; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.
